affordances.name: Scope ownership decision-rights filter
affordances.mechanism: When effort is diffusing across fuzzy scope, ownership, decision rights, or relevance criteria, require the answer to define what is inside, outside, influenceable, and worth protecting before adding work.

use_when: Scope, ownership, decision rights, or relevance criteria are fuzzy enough that effort is diffusing.
Saying no, narrowing the field, or refusing peripheral work creates more value than one more line of inquiry.
Finite time, attention, or political capital makes protecting the core objective more important than completeness.
do_not_use_when: A boundary is being drawn mainly to avoid hard conversations, accountability, or inconvenient evidence.
The answer would prematurely narrow inquiry before the problem frame has been tested.
The boundary hides feedback loops or interdependencies that cross the chosen perimeter.
case_evidence_needed: The current scope or ownership ambiguity.
What is inside, outside, or influenceable.
Evidence that exclusion serves the objective rather than comfort.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to define the boundary, name what it protects, and test whether excluded work is truly irrelevant or merely uncomfortable.
evidence_required: The capacity, role, or decision right at stake.
The excluded item and why it can be excluded.
good_output_shape: States what is inside the work.
States what is outside the work.
Names what can actually be influenced.
Checks whether the boundary protects purpose rather than comfort.

diagnostic_questions: What is inside, outside, and merely adjacent to the current objective?
Who owns the decision or action this answer is trying to assign?
Is this boundary protecting scarce capacity or avoiding an inconvenient issue?
misuse_guards: Do not draw scope lines to avoid accountability or evidence.
Do not narrow too early when multiple frames still need testing.
Do not hide cross-boundary dependencies behind a clean scope statement.

source_evidence.source_quote: scope, ownership, decision rights, or relevance criteria are fuzzy enough that energy is being wasted in every direction at once
Best when finite capacity matters more than completeness

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 7 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports a scope/ownership/decision-rights filter with comfort-protection and premature-narrowing guards.
dropped_material: Did not extract MECE, context-window, or Circle of Competence material as separate affordances.
Kept system-boundary theory as context for one compact packet card.
open_questions: Future packet review should test this card in cases where relationship boundaries and project scope boundaries collide.

absence_records.reason: The source warns that boundaries are misused when they protect comfort instead of serving the real objective.

source_evidence.source_quote: Danger When Boundaries Protect Comfort Instead of Purpose

absence_records.reason: The source explicitly identifies premature narrowing as a failure mode, not an affordance.

The source warns that tidy boundaries can hide feedback loops, interdependencies, and vital information outside the chosen frame. Do not promote a crisp scope line when the case evidence shows cross-boundary dependencies matter.

source_evidence.source_quote: A way of seeing is also a way of not seeing
artificially narrow system boundaries hide feedback loops and interdependencies that cross the defined perimeter

absence_records.reason: The source's only-enough-facts heuristic supports the current finite-capacity boundary and adjacent prioritization/leverage records; it should not become a separate Boundaries affordance unless the transaction is actually scope, ownership, relevance, or exclusion.

source_evidence.source_quote: What is the minimum set of facts needed to prove or disprove this specific hypothesis?

absence_records.reason: The source identifies the systems analyst trap: boundaries that become too large can obscure the answer. Do not promote ever-wider system mapping as Boundaries unless the wider perimeter improves the current decision.

source_evidence.source_quote: Systems analysts sometimes make boundaries too large
only serve to obscure the answers to the questions at hand

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 7 source reading found strong reviewed depth for Boundaries. Extracted one scope/ownership filter and rejected comfort-protection plus premature narrowing. PR98 keeps additional richness as absence rails for hidden cross-boundary dependencies, only-enough-facts split bloat, and overbroad system-boundary completeness.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet comparison should check whether this card helps receivers make crisp exclusions without hiding obligations.
